notes: Brazil pension reform July 1991. First post-reform PNAD is 1992.
RCS design - PNAD cross-sections. No individual panel ID.
TREAT = rural worker (rw_p5==1), control = urban worker (uwb2==1).
No PNAD in 1991 or 1994 - gaps in time series.
No weights used in original regressions.
Single treatment date - all treated units get treatment in 1992.

AUDIT NOTE (2026-04-19): Paper-auditor WITHIN_TOLERANCE (2.96% — rounding; paper reports 3 decimals and our -0.00776 rounds to -0.008). TREAT is load-bearing group dummy (rural indicator), NOT a robustness control — dropping it from TWFE produces sign reversal (+0.035) because TREAT absorbs baseline rural-urban fertility differential. So Spec B (no-ctrls TWFE) is a different estimand, not a robustness check. Spec A (CS+1 ctrl where the 1 ctrl IS the group dummy) collapses to 0/NA — this is collinearity, not classical Pattern 42 propensity overfit. CS-NT no-ctrls=-0.014 (81% larger than TWFE with ctrls) is directionally consistent but inflated; probably reflects omitted TREAT baseline differential appearing as treatment effect. 26 state clusters borderline for asymptotic inference. MODERATE rating justified.
